notes: A-Math 4049, E-Math 4052 full strands, Lower Sec 16 topics, Primary Math P1–P6, Combined Science, Physics 6091 gaps.

entries.subject: Mathematics
entries.syllabusRef: SEAB 4049 A-Math A1–A2 Quadratic functions and equations
keyConcepts: Complete the square for max/min of quadratic
Discriminant conditions for roots and line-curve intersection
Quadratic inequalities on number line; simultaneous equations with one linear
diagramCaptions: Parabola with vertex from completing square
Line tangent to curve when discriminant = 0

entries.subject: Mathematics
entries.syllabusRef: SEAB 4049 A3–A4 Surds and polynomials
keyConcepts: Simplify surds; rationalise denominators
Remainder and factor theorems; solve cubic equations
Partial fractions for proper rational expressions

entries.subject: Mathematics
entries.syllabusRef: SEAB 4049 A5–A6 Binomial expansions and exponential/logarithmic functions
keyConcepts: Binomial expansion for positive integer n
Laws of logarithms; change of base
Graphs of a^x and log_a x; solve simple exponential/log equations

entries.subject: Mathematics
entries.syllabusRef: SEAB 4049 G1 Trigonometric functions, identities and equations
keyConcepts: Six trig functions for any angle (degrees and radians)
Pythagorean and addition identities; double-angle formulae
Solve trigonometric equations in given intervals
diagramCaptions: Unit circle with sine and cosine
Trig graph showing period and amplitude

entries.subject: Mathematics
entries.syllabusRef: SEAB 4049 G2–G3 Coordinate geometry and plane proofs
keyConcepts: Equation of circle; intersection of line and circle
Tangents and normals to curves (calculus link)
Plane geometry proofs using congruence and similarity
diagramCaptions: Circle with tangent perpendicular to radius

entries.subject: Mathematics
entries.syllabusRef: SEAB 4049 C1 Differentiation
keyConcepts: Derivative as gradient and rate of change
Product, quotient and chain rules
Stationary points, max/min; tangents and normals
diagramCaptions: Tangent line at point on curve
Curve with maxima and minima labelled

entries.subject: Mathematics
entries.syllabusRef: SEAB 4049 C1 Integration and applications
keyConcepts: Indefinite and definite integrals; area under curve
Kinematics: v = ds/dt, a = dv/dt; displacement from velocity
Rate of change problems in context
diagramCaptions: Shaded area between curve and x-axis

entries.subject: Mathematics
entries.syllabusRef: SEAB 4052 N1–N4 Number, ratio, percentage, rate
keyConcepts: Prime factorisation; HCF and LCM
Direct and inverse proportion; map scales
Percentage increase/decrease and reverse percentages
Average speed; unit conversion km/h to m/s
diagramCaptions: Ratio bar model for sharing quantities

entries.subject: Mathematics
entries.syllabusRef: SEAB 4052 N5–N7 Algebraic expressions, functions, equations
keyConcepts: Expand and factorise linear and quadratic expressions
Solve linear and simultaneous equations
Sketch linear and quadratic graphs; gradient and intercept
diagramCaptions: Quadratic graph with roots from factorisation

entries.subject: Mathematics
entries.syllabusRef: SEAB 4052 N8–N9 Sets and matrices
keyConcepts: Set language: union, intersection, complement
Venn diagrams for two and three sets
Matrix addition, multiplication (2×2); real-life tabular data
diagramCaptions: Venn diagram with three overlapping sets

entries.subject: Mathematics
entries.syllabusRef: SEAB 4052 G2–G3 Congruence, similarity and circles
keyConcepts: Congruence tests (SSS, SAS, ASA, RHS); similar figures
Circle symmetry: equal chords, perpendicular bisector
Angle in semicircle; tangent perpendicular to radius; cyclic quadrilateral angles
diagramCaptions: Tangent-radius right angle at point of contact

entries.subject: Science
entries.syllabusRef: MOE Lower Secondary Science Topics 2–4 Diversity
keyConcepts: Classify matter by physical properties (density, melting point)
Elements, compounds and mixtures by chemical composition
Filtration, evaporation, distillation, chromatography for separation
diagramCaptions: Particle diagrams for element vs compound

entries.subject: Science
entries.syllabusRef: MOE Lower Secondary Science Topics 5–8 Models
keyConcepts: Ray model of light: reflection and refraction
Cell as basic unit; organelles and functions
Particulate model of matter; atoms and molecules introduction
diagramCaptions: Labelled cell model comparing plant and animal

entries.subject: Science
entries.syllabusRef: MOE Lower Secondary Science Topics 9–10 Interactions
keyConcepts: Work = force × distance; energy transfer and conservation
Conduction, convection, radiation
Effects of heat on matter and living things
diagramCaptions: Energy transfer diagram for roller coaster
Heat transfer in metal rod by conduction

entries.subject: Science
entries.syllabusRef: MOE Lower Secondary Science Topics 11–12
keyConcepts: Signs of chemical change; word equations
Acids and alkalis; neutralisation introduction
Food webs; human impact on ecosystems
diagramCaptions: Food web in local pond ecosystem

entries.subject: Science
entries.syllabusRef: MOE Lower Secondary Science Topics 13–16 Systems
keyConcepts: Series and parallel circuits; current and voltage
Human digestive system organs and enzymes
Transport in plants (xylem/phloem) and humans (blood vessels)
Human reproductive system overview

entries.subject: Mathematics
entries.syllabusRef: MOE Primary Mathematics 2021 — P1 to P3
keyConcepts: Place value up to 10 000; four operations
Mental calculation strategies; bar modelling introduction
Fractions as part of a whole; measurement of length, mass, volume
diagramCaptions: Bar model for addition/subtraction word problem

entries.subject: Mathematics
entries.syllabusRef: MOE Primary Mathematics 2021 — P4 to P6
keyConcepts: Factors, multiples, LCM, HCF; fractions four operations
Ratio, percentage, rate problems; average speed
Algebra: simple linear expressions and equations; PSLE problem-solving heuristics
diagramCaptions: Circle with radius, diameter, circumference labelled

entries.subject: Mathematics
entries.syllabusRef: MOE Primary Mathematics — Measurement and Geometry, Statistics
keyConcepts: Area of rectangle, triangle, parallelogram, trapezium; circumference and area of circle
Volume of cube and cuboid; nets of solids
Angles in triangles and quadrilaterals; pie charts and averages
diagramCaptions: Net of cuboid with surface area

entries.subject: Physics
entries.syllabusRef: MOE 6091 Topic 11 Electromagnetic Spectrum
keyConcepts: Order of EM spectrum by wavelength/frequency
Applications and hazards: microwave, UV, X-ray, gamma
All EM waves travel at same speed in vacuum

entries.subject: Physics
entries.syllabusRef: MOE 6091 Topic 8 Thermal Processes
keyConcepts: Conduction in solids; convection in fluids
Radiation does not need a medium; surfaces and colour affect emission/absorption
Everyday applications: vacuum flask, sea breeze
diagramCaptions: Comparison of three heat transfer modes

entries.subject: Physics
entries.syllabusRef: MOE 6091 Topic 7 Kinetic Particle Model of Matter
keyConcepts: States of matter explained by particle arrangement and motion
Gas pressure from collisions on walls; Boyle's law qualitative
Brownian motion as evidence for particles
diagramCaptions: Particle spacing in solid, liquid, gas
Gas molecules colliding with container walls

entries.subject: Physics
entries.syllabusRef: MOE 6091 Topic 6 Energy (2024 energy stores framework)
keyConcepts: Energy stored in kinetic, gravitational, elastic and thermal stores
Energy transferred by heating, waves, electrical, mechanical work
Conservation of energy; efficiency = useful output / total input
diagramCaptions: Roller coaster energy stores at each point

entries.subject: Biology
entries.syllabusRef: MOE 6093 Topic 13 Reproduction
keyConcepts: Asexual vs sexual reproduction; advantages and disadvantages
Human male and female reproductive systems
Menstrual cycle hormones; fertilisation and development overview

entries.subject: Biology
entries.syllabusRef: MOE 6093 Topic 12 Molecular Genetics
keyConcepts: DNA structure and complementary base pairing
Gene → protein: transcription and translation overview
Genetic engineering applications (insulin production, GM crops)
diagramCaptions: DNA double helix with base pairs

entries.subject: Science
entries.syllabusRef: SEAB 5086 Combined Science (Physics + Chemistry)
keyConcepts: Tests subset of 6091 Physics and 6092 Chemistry at reduced depth
Integrated practical skills across both disciplines
Cross-topic questions may link energy, electricity and chemical reactions
diagramCaptions: Practical planning for titration and circuits

entries.subject: Science
entries.syllabusRef: SEAB 5088 Combined Science (Chemistry + Biology)
keyConcepts: Tests subset of 6092 Chemistry and 6093 Biology
Topics may link biological molecules, enzymes and chemical tests
Ecology and air quality connect biology and chemistry contexts
diagramCaptions: Food test results linked to biological molecules

entries.subject: Chemistry
entries.syllabusRef: MOE 6092 §2 Atomic structure (Particulate Nature)
keyConcepts: Protons, neutrons, electrons; atomic and mass numbers
Isotopes same proton number different neutron number
Deduce subatomic particles in atoms and ions

entries.subject: Science
entries.syllabusRef: MOE Primary Science — early primary foundation
keyConcepts: Observe and compare living and non-living things
Basic senses and classification skills
Simple inquiry: ask questions, record observations with guidance
